Container.Name: vLLM ROCm
Container.Overview: OpenAI-compatible vLLM API server using the official ROCm image for AMD GPUs.
Container.Description:
vLLM ROCm runs the official vllm/vllm-openai-rocm container on Unraid for AMD GPU inference.
This template is intended for AMD ROCm-capable GPUs and exposes the OpenAI-compatible API on port 8000. It maps /dev/kfd and /dev/dri into the container and stores model/cache data under /root/.cache/huggingface.
The WebUI link points to the /v1/models API endpoint. vLLM itself is an API server, not a full chat web interface. For a browser chat UI, pair this with Open WebUI or another OpenAI-compatible frontend.
Start with a small model such as Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B, then adjust the Model Arguments field for larger models.
Container.Requires: AMD ROCm-capable GPU with /dev/kfd and /dev/dri available on the Unraid host. Radeon Top or equivalent AMD GPU host support may be required.

Config.Name: Model Storage
Config.Description: Stores Hugging Face model downloads and cache files.

Config.Name: Hugging Face Token
Config.Description: Optional Hugging Face token for gated or private models.

Config.Name: Model Arguments
Config.Description: Arguments passed to vLLM after the image name. Example: --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B --dtype auto --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 --max-model-len 8192
